summary: The conjecture asks whether for every graph $H$ and $\varepsilon > 0$, every $H$-free graph's vertex set can be covered (not necessarily partitioned) by a bounded number of $\varepsilon$-restricted subsets. The source paper (published in J. Combin. Theory Ser. B 163, 2023) explicitly identifies this as an open problem intermediate between their Theorem 1.3 and main Theorem 1.4, noting it does not obviously imply the stronger partition result. No follow-up paper resolving this conjecture was found in searches of the indexed literature through May 2026.
search_queries: Chudnovsky Scott Seymour Spirkl "Strengthening Rodl" epsilon-restricted H-free graph union cover 2105.07370
H-free graph "epsilon-restricted" union cover vertex partition Rodl theorem graph theory 2022 2023 2024
Chudnovsky Scott Seymour Spirkl "epsilon-restricted" OR "varepsilon-restricted" "union" H-free graph conjecture arxiv 2023 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up found. The paper appeared on arXiv in May 2021 and was published in J. Combin. Theory Ser. B 163:256–271 (2023). The conjecture is explicitly labelled open in the source paper, sitting between Theorem 1.3 (partition into weakly ε-restricted sets) and Theorem 1.4 (partition into ε-restricted sets); the authors remark it does not seem easy and does not obviously imply 1.4. Sophie Spirkl's arXiv listing shows no subsequent paper on this topic; Semantic Scholar citation data was inconclusive.
